frozen_components: BGE-M3 sentence embedding model (used to encode teacher CoTs)
architecture.summary: A two-stage framework for e-commerce relevance ranking: (1) Multi-Perspective CoT (MPCoT) teacher, a Qwen3-14B LLM fine-tuned via SFT on aggregated rationales from three reasoning perspectives (User Intent, Structured Analysis, Business Rules), then refined with DPO on conflicting cases to learn perspective selection. (2) Latent Reasoning Knowledge Distillation (LRKD) student, a BERT-multilingual-base cross-encoder augmented with a lightweight trainable latent reasoning extractor that maps BERT hidden states to a latent vector, aligned via MSE loss to frozen sentence embeddings of teacher CoTs, enabling compact deployment without rationale generation.
trained_components: Qwen3-14B LoRA adapters (SFT and DPO stages)
latent reasoning extractor (MLP, GAT, or Poly-Encoder variants)

biblio.title: Thinking Broad, Acting Fast: Latent Reasoning Distillation from Multi-Perspective Chain-of-Thought for E-Commerce Relevance

claims.statement: Our final teacher model (MPCoT SFT+DPO) surpasses the strongest single-perspective baseline by an average of +1.96 points in accuracy and +3.5 points in F1 across all datasets.

The multi-perspective oracle (three prompts with different perspectives, correct if any matches) achieved 77.52% accuracy, significantly outperforming the pass@3 single-perspective control (72.59%) and the single User Intent perspective (64.61%).
LRKD with GAT-based extractor improves accuracy by +2.37 points and F1 by +3.91 points on average over BERT-multilingual-base baseline.
LRKD with GAT outperforms MKD (Zhao et al., 2025) by +1.15 points in accuracy and +2.49 points in F1 across datasets.

claims.dataset: AliExpress live search advertising (tens of millions of daily users)
claims.statement: Online A/B test on AliExpress search advertising platform: +1.42% improvement in Revenue Per Mille (RPM), +0.48% increase in Click-Through Rate (CTR), and +0.4% increase in relevance satisfaction (RS) score.
The latent reasoning vector achieves an average F1 score of 0.403 on predicting top 15 non-trivial CoT keywords, an 81.8% relative improvement over [CLS] representation (0.221).

claims.dataset: synthetic benchmark (100 query–item pairs per batch)
claims.statement: Teacher model inference latency is 46,800 ms vs. student BERT baseline 132.22 ms and LRKD-GAT 148.76 ms (marginal +16.54 ms overhead) on NVIDIA A100 GPU for 100 query–item pairs per batch.

Removing the latent reasoning extractor (replacing with [CLS] representation) degrades performance by −1.49 to −1.73 accuracy points and −0.89 to −1.35 F1 points, indicating representation overload when a single vector must capture both classification and reasoning.
